examples.title: Cohen-Sutherland Region Code Assignment
examples.description: Algorithm to assign 4-bit region codes to endpoints
examples.code:
FUNCTION AssignRegionCode(x, y, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax):
 code = 0000
 IF y > ymax THEN code = code | 8 // 1000 - TOP
 IF y < ymin THEN code = code | 4 // 0100 - BOTTOM
 IF x > xmax THEN code = code | 2 // 0010 - RIGHT
 IF x < xmin THEN code = code | 1 // 0001 - LEFT
 RETURN code
examples.explanation: Each bit corresponds to a boundary. If point is beyond that boundary, the corresponding bit is set to 1. Bitwise OR accumulates multiple boundary violations.

examples.title: Cohen-Sutherland Line Clipping Main Algorithm
examples.description: Complete algorithm with trivial accept/reject and clipping logic
examples.code:
FUNCTION CohenSutherlandClip(x1, y1, x2, y2, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax):
 WHILE true DO:
 code1 = AssignRegionCode(x1, y1, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax)
 code2 = AssignRegionCode(x2, y2, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax)
 
 // Trivial Accept: both inside
 IF code1 = 0 AND code2 = 0 THEN
 RETURN (x1, y1, x2, y2) // Entire line visible
 
 // Trivial Reject: AND ≠ 0
 IF (code1 AND code2) ≠ 0 THEN
 RETURN NULL // Line completely outside
 
 // Need to clip: find intersection with boundary
 IF code1 = 0 THEN
 SWAP(x1, x2); SWAP(y1, y2); SWAP(code1, code2)
 
 // Calculate intersection with each edge
 IF code1 & 8 THEN // Above top
 x = x1 + (x2-x1)*(ymax-y1)/(y2-y1)
 y = ymax
 ELSE IF code1 & 4 THEN // Below bottom
 x = x1 + (x2-x1)*(ymin-y1)/(y2-y1)
 y = ymin
 ELSE IF code1 & 2 THEN // Right of right edge
 y = y1 + (y2-y1)*(xmax-x1)/(x2-x1)
 x = xmax
 ELSE // Left of left edge
 y = y1 + (y2-y1)*(xmin-x1)/(x2-x1)
 x = xmin
 
 // Update endpoint to intersection point
 x1 = x; y1 = y
 END WHILE
examples.explanation: The algorithm iteratively clips the line until it either becomes fully visible (trivial accept) or is determined to be outside (trivial reject). At each iteration, one endpoint is guaranteed to be outside, and the intersection with the appropriate boundary is computed.

examples.title: Liang-Barsky Line Clipping Algorithm
examples.description: Parametric approach for efficient rectangular line clipping
examples.code:
FUNCTION LiangBarskyClip(x1, y1, x2, y2, xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax):
 dx = x2 - x1
 dy = y2 - y1
 
 p1 = -dx; q1 = x1 - xmin // Left edge
 p2 = dx; q2 = xmax - x1 // Right edge
 p3 = -dy; q3 = y1 - ymin // Bottom edge
 p4 = dy; q4 = ymax - y1 // Top edge
 
 p = [p1, p2, p3, p4]
 q = [q1, q2, q3, q4]
 
 t0 = 0.0; t1 = 1.0
 
 FOR i = 0 TO 3 DO:
 IF p[i] = 0 THEN:
 IF q[i] < 0 THEN RETURN NULL // Parallel and outside
 ELSE:
 r = q[i] / p[i]
 IF p[i] < 0 THEN:
 IF r > t1 THEN RETURN NULL
 t0 = MAX(t0, r)
 ELSE: // p[i] > 0
 IF r < t0 THEN RETURN NULL
 t1 = MIN(t1, r)
 
 // Calculate clipped endpoints
 IF t0 > t1 THEN RETURN NULL // No visible portion
 
 xc1 = x1 + t0 * dx
 yc1 = y1 + t0 * dy
 xc2 = x1 + t1 * dx
 yc2 = y1 + t1 * dy
 
 RETURN (xc1, yc1, xc2, yc2)
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses parametric form. For each edge, it computes t values where the line enters (t0) and exits (t1) the clip window. If after processing all edges t0 > t1, the line is invisible. Otherwise, the clipped coordinates are computed using the final t0 and t1 values.

examples.title: Intersection Point Calculation
examples.description: Helper function to find intersection of line with vertical or horizontal boundary
examples.code:
FUNCTION GetIntersection(x1, y1, x2, y2, edge, boundary):
 // edge: 'left', 'right', 'top', 'bottom'
 // boundary: xmin, xmax, ymax, ymin value
 
 dx = x2 - x1
 dy = y2 - y1
 
 IF edge = 'left' OR edge = 'right' THEN:
 IF dy = 0 THEN RETURN NULL // Horizontal line parallel
 t = (boundary - x1) / dx
 y = y1 + t * dy
 RETURN (boundary, y)
 
 IF edge = 'top' OR edge = 'bottom' THEN:
 IF dx = 0 THEN RETURN NULL // Vertical line parallel
 t = (boundary - y1) / dy
 x = x1 + t * dx
 RETURN (x, boundary)
examples.explanation: This helper calculates where a line segment crosses a specific boundary. Uses the line equation in parametric form. Returns the intersection point coordinates or NULL if line is parallel to the boundary.
